name: An Evening at the Ardmore Gallery
description: A short dating-sim vignette set at a private art-gallery opening. Over the course of one evening the player navigates desire, wit, and rivalry — choosing who to charm, who to confide in, and whether to leave alone or with someone special.
intent: showcase relationships with multiple axes, an attached romance-stage machine, derived social-graph queries, guarded beats, and three differentiated endings

evening_over.description: Set true when the player steps toward the door

character.description: A person attending the gallery opening

confidence.description: How self-assured this person feels tonight

secrets_shared.description: Topics this character has confided to the player

location.description: An area of the Ardmore gallery

velvet_jacket.description: A tailored velvet jacket — the kind that makes strangers want to know your name.

romance.description: A romantic/social connection between two people at the gallery

tension.description: Charged friction — can be attractive or destabilising

trust.description: Confidence that the other person is genuine

admirer_count.intent: how many NPCs have developed meaningful affection for the player

top_admirer.intent: the NPC with the highest affection for the player

tension_sources.intent: NPCs whose tension with the player is high enough to be a plot force

arc.description: The narrative arc of the evening — movement through the gallery and endings

arrival.description: You step through the glass doors into a warm blaze of light and low jazz. Tonight, anything is possible.

cocktails.description: Guests cluster at the bar and around tall tables strewn with catalogues. Introductions are being made.

gallery_floor.description: The main gallery opens up — paintings ablaze with colour, champagne flutes catching the light. You move among the crowd.

private_room.description: A quieter anteroom, lit by a single recessed lamp. Someone has followed you in.

left_together.description: You leave through the gallery's front door together — coat sleeves just touching — into a city that suddenly feels full of possibility.
left_together.intent: reached when one NPC has affection >= 70 and trust >= 50

left_alone.description: You button your coat at the kerb and hail a cab alone. The evening was beautiful, the art was beautiful — and none of it quite reached you. Next time, perhaps.
left_alone.intent: reached when the player exits with no NPC at high enough affection

love_triangle.description: Two people catch your eye from across the foyer at the same moment — and catch each other's eyes too. The three of you stand in a charged, impossible triangle. Nobody moves first. You will think about this evening for a long time.
love_triangle.intent: reached when two or more NPCs both have affection >= 60 and tension >= 40

transitions.description: Make your way to the bar and join the early crowd.

effects.text: You drift toward the bar. Laughter and clattering ice-cubes fill the air.

transitions.description: Slip away from the bar into the main gallery.

effects.text: The paintings swallow you. Someone nearby says something clever about the brushwork.

transitions.description: Slip into the quiet anteroom at the back of the gallery.

effects.text: The noise fades behind a heavy curtain. The room smells of cedar and old canvas.

transitions.description: Leave the gallery with your closest admirer.

effects.text: You leave together. The city opens up before you both.

transitions.description: Step toward the door when the room is electrically charged with competing desire.

effects.text: The triangle holds. No one breaks first.

transitions.description: Head for the door — the evening is what it was.

effects.text: You leave alone. A good evening. A quiet cab. Your thoughts your own.

romance_stage.description: Per-relationship stage machine — each romance arc carries its own state

transitions.description: Strike up a first real conversation.

effects.text: A first real exchange. Something clicks into place.

transitions.description: Enough warmth has built — you're no longer strangers.

effects.text: Something clicks into place — you're no longer strangers.

transitions.description: Lean into the charged subtext between you.

effects.text: You lean into the subtext. The response is encouraging.

transitions.description: Tell them something true about yourself.

effects.text: You say the real thing. They listen in a way that feels rare.

transitions.description: The dynamic tilts unmistakably into flirtation.

effects.text: The subtext becomes text. There's a charge in the air.

transitions.description: Open up about something that matters to you.

effects.text: The conversation goes somewhere neither of you expected.

transitions.description: Turn on the full charm — this needs a certain confidence.

effects.text: Full charm deployed. They forget to keep their guard up.

transitions.description: One of you has clearly fallen.

effects.text: It's unmistakable now. Possibly embarrassing. Definitely real.
The party gathers around the bar.
The guests drift out onto the gallery floor.
You slip away to the quiet of the private room.

celeste_opens_up.text: After a pause, Celeste says: 'You know, I almost didn't come tonight. I'm glad I did.' She doesn't explain further. She doesn't need to.
celeste_opens_up.intent: fires when trust with Celeste crosses 20

dominic_first_meeting.text: Dominic Reyes — you've read his name on the catalogue. He's been watching the room with the careful attention of someone who arranged it.
dominic_first_meeting.intent: introduces Dominic; fires once he is first engaged with

triangle_forming.text: Both Celeste and Rafael catch your eye from opposite sides of the room at the same moment. Neither looks away first.
triangle_forming.intent: fires when both Celeste and Rafael are in the 40+ affection zone — triangle crystallises

private_room_moment.text: In the hush of the anteroom, the sounds of the party become irrelevant. Whatever is happening in here is the only thing happening.
private_room_moment.intent: ambient beat — repeatable while in the private room

smitten_threshold.text: You realise with a small shock that you've stopped noticing the art.
smitten_threshold.intent: fires when any NPC's affection crosses 60

ardmore_history.title: The Ardmore Gallery
ardmore_history.text: Founded in 1987 by Miriam Ardmore, a retired set designer who converted a Victorian printworks. The gallery has a reputation for championing artists who make people uncomfortable in productive ways.

celeste_background.title: Celeste Vann
celeste_background.text: A poet and essayist who writes about grief with unusual precision. Three published collections and a habit of saying the true thing at the wrong moment.
celeste_background.subject: celeste

rafael_background.title: Rafael Osei
rafael_background.text: An architect turned critic who has been arguing publicly with the art establishment for a decade. Brilliant, occasionally exhausting, always sincere.
rafael_background.subject: rafael
rafael_background.intent: positions Rafael's combativeness as coming from genuine passion

dominic_background.title: Dominic Reyes
dominic_background.text: Curated the Ardmore for five years before anyone noticed him. Quiet in public, volcanic in private. The evening's hanging was his concept.
dominic_background.subject: dominic
dominic_background.intent: explains Dominic's reticence and rewards the player who seeks him out

player.description: A writer who accepted this invitation on impulse and is not entirely sure why.

celeste.description: Stands near a large abstract oil, glass of red wine at her lips, reading the label rather than the painting.

rafael.description: Talks with his hands. Currently holding court in front of a bronze sculpture, champagne forgotten on the plinth behind him.

dominic.description: Stands near the back, slightly apart, watching the crowd with the intensity of someone who arranged it.

the_bar.description: A long marble bar running the length of the entrance room.

the_gallery.description: The main exhibition space — high ceilings, bold canvases, champagne flutes catching the light.

the_terrace.description: A narrow stone terrace overlooking the street, used for fresh air and quiet conversations.

the_private_room.description: A quieter anteroom at the back, lit by a single recessed lamp.

mingling.record: The party gathers around the bar.

on_the_floor.record: The guests drift out onto the gallery floor.

alone_together.record: You slip away to the quiet of the private room.
